page: The UpCloud 38xCPU-192GB is a general-purpose server powered by the KVM hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. This x86_64 architecture configuration features 38 physical cores and 38 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache sizes. It supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, but lacks AVX-512 extensions. The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 5.05 GB of memory per core, and includes a bundled 2048 GB local SSD alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-threaded capabilities. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the bottom 10% (745.2) and Geekbench single-core scoring in the bottom 25% (1096.0). However, the high core count compensates in parallelized workloads; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores both rank in the middle 50%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly in the top 25% (278.42 GB/sec), and PassMark CPU encryption also reaches the top 25% (38869.56). Conversely, PassMark memory performance is poor, falling into the bottom 10% (1129.14), and large-model LLM text generation using a 7B model is also limited to the bottom 10% (5.77 tokens/sec).

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of physical cores and a substantial 2048 GB local SSD storage allocation, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core frequency or extreme memory speeds. The resource profile is well-suited for parallel processing tasks, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving (which achieves an average throughput of 19.8 GB/sec), and standard database operations. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications, memory-bound computations, or complex LLM text generation.
description: The UpCloud 38xCPU-192GB is a general-purpose server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 38 physical cores and 38 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, running at 2.0 GHz with 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2048 GB local SSD. While single-core performance is limited, ranking in the bottom 10% for stress-ng, its multi-core capabilities are robust, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU encryption both placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain average, though PassMark memory scores fall into the bottom 10%. This server is best suited for highly parallelized workloads, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving, rather than single-threaded or memory-intensive applications.
og_description: UpCloud 38xCPU-192GB is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 38 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2 TB SSD, delivering strong multi-threaded performance despite limited single-core speeds.
meta_description: UpCloud 38xCPU-192GB is an AMD EPYC-powered KVM server with 38 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD storage for parallelized workloads.
tagline: A multi-core x86_64 server with 192 GB RAM and 2 TB SSD storage, optimized for highly parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 38 physical cores and 38 vCPUs.
Includes 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2048 GB local SSD.
Strong multi-threaded performance, ranking in the top 25% for bzip3 decompression.
Weak single-core performance, falling into the bottom 10% on stress-ng benchmarks.
Well-suited for parallel data processing, static web serving, and multi-threaded workloads.
